name: aya
readme_summary: AYA is a desktop application that provides a graphical user interface for controlling Android devices via ADB. It simplifies common development and debugging tasks by offering a visual wrapper around the command-line ADB tool.
primary_use_case: Providing a user-friendly desktop GUI for managing and debugging Android devices connected via ADB.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot arm acting as a conductor, orchestrating a symphony of digital elements emanating from a sleek, modern Android phone. The robot arm is smoothly manipulating glowing data streams and control panels, representing the ADB commands. The phone displays a vibrant, interactive interface mirroring the robot's actions. Subtle UI elements and code snippets float around the scene, indicating the software's nature. The background is a clean, minimalist workspace, suggesting efficiency and ease of use. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, optimistic color palette, emphasizing the seamless control and accessibility AYA provides.

name: openserp
description: Self-hosted SERP API for AI, SEO & automation. Browser-rendered Google, Bing, Yandex, Baidu, DuckDuckGo and Ecosia search with page extraction 🎉
readme_summary: OpenSERP is an open-source, self-hostable API and CLI that provides live search data from multiple search engines like Google, Yandex, and Bing. It's designed for AI, SEO, and automation workflows, offering features like URL extraction, SERP feature parsing, and various data output formats.
key_features: Multi-engine support (Google, Yandex, Baidu, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Ecosia)
URL extraction with content parsing (Markdown, Text)
SERP features (AI summaries, answer boxes, etc.)
Advanced filters (language, date, file type, site)
Multiple data formats (JSON, Markdown, Text, NdJSON)
Configurable proxies, cache, and resilient mode
primary_use_case: Providing a self-hosted, multi-engine Search Engine Results Page (SERP) API for AI, LLM, RAG, and SEO automation tasks, especially for non-Google search engine coverage.
cover_image_prompt: A digital librarian meticulously organizing and extracting information from a vast, interconnected library of search engine results pages. The librarian is shown holding a magnifying glass over a glowing 'Google' book, while simultaneously pulling data streams from 'Bing', 'Yandex', and 'Baidu' books into a neat, organized digital briefcase labeled 'OpenSERP'. Small icons representing AI, SEO, and automation float around the scene, indicating the diverse applications. The background is a clean, modern interface with subtle code snippets and data visualizations. The overall mood is efficient and intelligent, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, tech-focused color palette.
